analysis.test: Stress-testing the adverse party's copyright defense: Méridian Studios must counter three lines of defense — (i) lack of originality of Bastille (idea/expression dichotomy, art. L112-1 CPI), (ii) priority/independence of the exploitation of Tohu-Bohu relative to Bastille, (iii) lack of standing of Méridian Studios for want of a valid assignment deed (art. L131-3 CPI). The test is: does Méridian Studios establish, as against each […], (a) the originality of the concrete expression of Bastille beyond generic game mechanics, (b) ownership or valid assignment of the economic rights, and (c) reproduction of protectable elements in Tohu-Bohu? Primary legal basis: art. L122-4 CPI (act of infringement without authorization); assignment defense: art. L131-3 CPI.
faits: Méridian Studios is the registrant of Bastille and exploits that trade name (chart), but the e-mail […] → Méridian Studios dated 01/09/2019 does not specify whether the transfer covers the economic rights of authorship or merely the business goodwill.

The judgment of the TJ Paris of 18 December 2020 (Cassiopée v. Méridian Studios, Exhibit 12, adverse party) has already ruled on a dispute […] the same […] parties concerning similar games, and Exhibit 5 (official letter of 05/10/2021) recalls that Méridian Studios had itself invoked 'a common current shared with many games' — an argument that Cassiopée now turns against it.
Opposing Exhibit 3 (other games with a concept identical to Bastille) and opposing Exhibit 13 (CA Paris, Karbone 14) are aimed at reducing Bastille to a non-protectable generic idea.

Exhibits 3 Ter, 14.1, and 14.2 (ours) document the priority of Bluff & Cie (2013) and the difference in concrete expression […] Bastille and games of the genre, which […] serves both to establish the specific originality of Bastille and to distinguish the expressions.
analysis.faille: Absence of a copyright assignment deed for the economic rights in Bastille formally compliant with art. L131-3 (express mention of each right assigned, scope, intended use, duration, territory) in the e-mail […] → Méridian Studios dated 01/09/2019; risk of inadmissibility of the copyright infringement action if Méridian Studios cannot establish a valid assignment or original authorship status.
analysis.riposte: On standing: produce the duly formalized assignment agreement or demonstrate that Méridian Studios is itself the author of the concrete expression of Bastille (collective work or work made for hire). On originality: rely on Exhibits 3 Ter, 14.1, and 14.2 to demonstrate that the concrete expression of Bastille (card wording, challenge structure, graphic charter) is distinct and original as compared with Bluff & Cie and generic games — turn the 'common current' argument against the opposing party: when Méridian Studios invoked it in the Cassiopée v. Méridian Studios matter, it was to distinguish Bastille from Carto Royale 4, not to deny the originality of Bastille itself.
analysis.pourquoi: The email […] → Méridian Studios of 01/09/2019 does not expressly mention the economic rights in the copyright or their scope (duration, territory, […] of exploitation), as required by Article L. 131-3 CPI. In the absence of a formally compliant assignment instrument, Cassiopée may raise the inadmissibility of Méridian Studios's copyright infringement action, supported by the holding in Cass. civ. 1, 22 mars 2012 [2] […] which requires proof of residual or assigned economic rights.

autorites.title: L131-3 — Code de la propriété intellectuelle
Cass. civ. 1, no. 11-10.132, March 22, 2012
themes: personne physique ou morale à l'initiative de l'oeuvre collective
autorites.summary: C'est sans inverser la charge de la preuve qu'une cour d'appel, ayant constaté que les […] revendiquées par la société agissant en contrefaçon de droits d'auteur avaient fait l'objet de contrats de cession de droits au profit de tiers, en a déduit que, faute pour la société de fournir un quelconque élément propre à établir l'existence de droits patrimoniaux résiduels dont elle serait restée investie, celle-ci était irrecevable à agir du chef de la protection de ces droits

autorites.title: Cass. civ. 1, no. 06-13.342, April 3, 2007
themes: auteur ayant cédé ses droits patrimoniaux
garantie due à l'éditeur de l'exercice paisible et exclusif du droit cédé
droit au respect du nom de l'auteur
reproduction totale ou partielle de l'oeuvre d'autrui en s'en appropriant la paternité
autorites.summary: Ayant constaté qu'un auteur avait cédé à un éditeur la totalité de ses droits patrimoniaux, sans se réserver la possibilité de poursuivre les tiers contrefacteurs en raison des atteintes […] y seraient éventuellement portées, une cour d'appel le déclare à bon droit irrecevable à agir en contrefaçon sur le fondement de ces droits, peu important que ceux-ci aient été cédés en contrepartie d'une rémunération proportionnelle

analysis.acte_referme: Copyright assignment deed in […] proper form signed by Raphaël Dumas (original author or successor in title) in favor of Méridian Studios, expressly identifying the rights of reproduction, public performance, and adaptation, for the full statutory duration, all territories, all media including digital — to be regularized or produced if it already exists.
analysis.moyen_adverse: Cassiopée contends that Méridian Studios lacks standing to bring an infringement action for want of a properly executed assignment of the economic rights in Bastille (art. L131-3 CPI), and that even if an assignment existed, Bastille is not entitled to any protection because it reduces to a generic idea for a sentence-based board game, rendered commonplace by the state of the art (opposing Exhibits 3 and 13).
analysis.these_adverse: Cassiopée argues, relying on Exhibits 3 and 13, that Bastille is merely a variant of a generic concept for a sentence-based game already exploited by numerous publishers, thereby reducing Méridian Studios' claim to a non-protectable idea within the meaning of art. L112-1 CPI, as held by the CA Paris in Karbone 14. Cassiopée further exploits Méridian Studios' prior position in the TJ Paris 2020 litigation — in which Méridian Studios had itself invoked a 'common current shared with many games' — to demonstrate the absence of distinctive originality in Bastille. Finally, Cassiopée challenges Méridian Studios' standing in the absence of a copyright assignment instrument for the economic rights formally compliant with art. L131-3 CPI, relying on Cass. civ. 1, March 22, 2012 [2] to obtain a ruling of inadmissibility of the action.

pieces_adverses.piece: Exhibit 3 – Other games with a concept identical to Bastille (adverse party's .docx document)
pieces_adverses.contre: Uncertified document, bearing no certified date and no verifiable source; the games listed do not reproduce the concrete expression of Bastille but only the generic concept — the idea/expression dichotomy must be raised in opposition

pieces_adverses.piece: Exhibit 13 – CA Paris, Karbone 14 (adverse party's judgment)
pieces_adverses.contre: Non-transposable precedent: the Karbone 14 case concerned a game title and not the expression of cards/challenges; the elements of originality in Bastille (wording, structure, graphic design) are distinct from those at issue in that decision

pieces_adverses.piece: Exhibit 5 – Official letter from Méridian Studios dated 10/05/2021 (adverse party)
pieces_adverses.contre: The 'common current' argument was contextualized to the Bastille/Carto Royale 4 comparison in the 2020 litigation; it does not constitute a general acknowledgment of the absence of originality of Bastille — the reversal of that argument is a fallacy of decontextualization.

pieces_attendues: Copyright assignment deed signed by Raphaël Dumas in favor of Méridian Studios, compliant with art. L131-3 CPI (rights, scope, duration, territory)
Witness statement or declaration by Raphaël Dumas acknowledging his status as original author of Bastille and the validity of the assignment
Detailed comparative table of the Bastille cards/challenges versus games of the genre (Bluff & Cie, Carto Royale 4, Concerto 2) demonstrating the originality of the concrete expression
Bailiff's report (constat d'huissier) or certified capture of the distinctive elements of Bastille (card wording, graphic design, structure) predating Tohu-Bohu
